name: immigration_policy_municipal_bond_redwood_rig_utl
premise: A Immigration Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving immigration policy municipal bond redwood rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 45 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Kaito Kim
agents.identity: I am Kaito Kim, accountable for a defensible call in Immigration Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Keisha Navarro
agents.identity: I am Keisha Navarro, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Immigration Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 45 minutes. Evidence is partial. Kaito Kim must decide under rights vs utility. What does Kaito Kim do?
